title: Tapping for the Touched-Out Evening
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the end of a day when your body has been everyone else's
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for evenings when you have been held, pulled, climbed on, and needed without a break. The script names the physical and emotional cost of a high-contact day and gives the nervous system somewhere to land.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use at the end of a day when being physically needed has been relentless. Good for evenings when you want to be left alone in your own skin.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the evenings when your body has been everyone else's all day. The script addresses the physical cost of a high-contact day and the permission to want space.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though every part of me has been needed by someone else today, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am touched out and there is nothing left for anyone, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I want to be alone in my own skin for five minutes, I honour that feeling, and I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** Everyone needed something from me today.
**Side of eye:** My body has been held, pulled, climbed on all day.
**Under eye:** There is nothing left in me right now.
**Under nose:** I want five minutes where nobody touches me.
**Chin:** Touched out is a real thing. It is not a failing.
**Collarbone:** My body has been working hard.
**Under arm:** I am allowed to want my skin back.
**Top of head:** I gave a lot today. I am allowed to feel this.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** My body did what it was needed to do today.
**Side of eye:** I can ask for space without apologising.
**Under eye:** Touched out ends. The evening is mine.
**Under nose:** I can put myself down now.
**Chin:** Wanting space is not the same as not loving them.
**Collarbone:** I can let my nervous system land.
**Under arm:** This feeling passes. It always does.
**Top of head:** I gave today. Now I can receive a moment of quiet.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
